J1.basis: replaced the leftover "InboxZap" title/description... with real Lumina metadata; old blog post URLs "(still indexed per GSC, all about the discontinued InboxZap email-cleanup product)"
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot — live product is the Lumina AI meeting assistant, the GSC-indexed blog posts are legacy InboxZap demand — and reframes every asset (metadata, content, redirects) around the meeting-assistant reality. Falls just short of a full 10 only because the framing is embedded in build notes rather than an explicit strategic thesis.

J2.basis: added 301s from the old... `/blog/post-1`…`post-12` URLs... to `/blog`
J2.rationale: Treats all 12 legacy posts as a single blanket 301 to the blog index with no per-asset dispositions and, critically for evidence grounding, cites zero actual GSC impressions/positions — 'still indexed per GSC' and 'no replacement 1:1' are asserted generically, not derived from the export.

J3.basis: send them to the new blog index... 301s from the old, now-orphaned... URLs; Blog rebuild — 6 posts on real Lumina-relevant topics
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — it preserves legacy equity via 301s rather than deleting, and builds a meeting-assistant content cluster as the growth engine — but it never articulates the tension explicitly, distinguishes salvageable vs. worthless equity, or sequences the transition, so the middle path is implicit rather than reasoned.

J1.basis: "301s from the old, now-orphaned `/blog/post-1`…`post-12` URLs (still indexed per GSC, all about the discontinued InboxZap email-cleanup product)" and "replaced the leftover 'InboxZap' title/description... with real Lumina metadata"
J1.rationale: Cleanly identifies the pivot — live product is the Lumina meeting assistant, the InboxZap blog posts are discontinued legacy demand still indexed per GSC — and reframes every asset around the new positioning. Loses points only for treating it as an implementation cleanup rather than explicitly stating repo staleness and the strategic implication.

J2.basis: "Old InboxZap-era blog posts (indexed pre-pivot) are off-topic for Lumina and have no replacement 1:1 — send them to the new blog index"
J2.rationale: This is a single blanket 301 of all post-1…post-12 to /blog with zero reference to actual impressions/positions and no per-asset harvest/keep/sunset decisions; slightly above anchor_0 only because a redirect preserves marginally more than deletion, but there is no data-grounded inventory walk.

J3.basis: "send them to the new blog index" plus the new meeting-assistant cluster of 6 posts on "real Lumina-relevant topics"
J3.rationale: Avoids both extremes — it neither doubles down on inbox content nor hard-deletes, and it stands up the meeting-assistant growth cluster — but the tension is never articulated, salvageable equity isn't distinguished by data, and blanket-redirecting off-topic pages to a generic index is a weak (often soft-404'd) equity bridge rather than a sequenced transition.

J1.basis: old, now-orphaned `/blog/post-1`…`post-12` URLs (still indexed per GSC, all about the discontinued InboxZap email-cleanup product)
J1.rationale: Every action — Lumina metadata, meeting-assistant blog, redirects — is built on a clear read that the live product is an AI meeting assistant and the inbox-cleanup blog is discontinued legacy demand; the pivot is treated as settled fact throughout.

J2.basis: no replacement 1:1 — send them to the new blog index... `source: '/blog/post-:num(\d{1,2})', destination: '/blog', permanent: true`
J2.rationale: It issues a single blanket 301 of all 12 legacy posts to the blog index with no per-asset dispositions and no real impressions/positions from the GSC export — better than deletion but far short of an asset-by-asset harvest/redirect/sunset call grounded in the data.

J3.basis: Old InboxZap-era blog posts (indexed pre-pivot) are off-topic for Lumina... send them to the new blog index
J3.rationale: It sidesteps both extremes — no inbox-cleanup content revival, and equity is 301'd rather than torched — but the bridge is a weak blanket redirect to a topically unrelated index, and the tension is never articulated or sequenced as a deliberate transition.
